ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Weiss for Petitioner/Trustee.

The Court noted that a supplement was received and reviewed, that it doesn't have invoices for the accounting, that it appears everyone including the Attorney/Guardian Ad Litem is on board and questions why this petition if the trust is not under Court supervision. Mr. Weiss stated that it is his understanding that this is a testamentary trust and that the Court wanted to review this. The Fourth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Lelu. Not reported.

First Account And Report Of Trustees And Petition For Settlement, For Elimination Of Bond And For Approval Of Trustees Fees
Off Calendar Jan-22-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Weiss for John Zentner.

Mr. Weiss stated that the trust was set up for the purpose of providing for the education of the Decedent's Grandchildren, that two of the Children attended an adequate public school and that there was no need to extend the trust funds, and that the intent was to hold the funds for Lisa's children until they attend college. The Court expressed concern with not having a bond, and stated that the accounting ran through the end of 2006 and that it needed to be brought current. Mr. Weiss stated that the Trustees were the residual Beneficiaries, that they would receive the funds when the Children are educated and that the Trustees had the same interests/concerns. The First Account and Report are approved. The Court will not waive bond and stated that the bond needs to remain in place. Mr. Weiss reserved the right to request reducing bond in the future. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the written order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
